candidates.why: Component of operating revenue but not the overall top-line; a more inclusive total revenue line exists.

candidates.row_label: Revenue from sale of repossessed assets
candidates.why: Specific revenue component; not the comprehensive revenue figure.
Explicit aggregate revenue line capturing all revenue components; best representation of top-line sales.
Finance/interest income, not total operating revenue; should not be used as primary top-line when Total revenue exists.
Component of financial revenue only; not overall revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Earnings measure, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income/(loss) for the period
candidates.why: Comprehensive income, far below revenue line in statement hierarchy.
Balance sheet asset, not revenue.
Liability, not revenue.

variables.notes: Total revenue is clearly labeled and numerically includes the various revenue components (interest revenue from purchased loan portfolios, revenue from sale of repossessed assets, other operating revenue). No evidence of an alternative top-line label, so a single priority row suffices.

candidates.why: Could contain both direct and overhead costs; however, a more specifically labeled direct cost line exists (Cost of Revenues - Other).
Residual operating expenses; likely overhead rather than pure direct costs.
Would usually be direct costs, but appears empty in preview; still, label is generic and not clearly used here.
Specific overhead operating expense, not general COGS.
Explicitly labeled as cost of revenues; best proxy for COGS/direct costs.
Likely an overhead staffing cost related to collections, not direct production/service COGS.
General personnel overhead; generally classified as SG&A rather than COGS for financial institutions.
Same concept as other operating expenses; overhead bucket, not clearly direct cost only.
Net operating result, includes revenues and expenses; not a pure cost of sales line.
Financing costs, not cost of goods sold.
Financing costs, not cost of goods sold.
Would typically be COGS, but appears unused here; also company is a credit/collections business, so raw materials are unlikely to be core COGS.
Overhead operating cost, not direct cost of revenues.
Liability, not an income statement cost line.
Liability category; not a specific COGS line.

variables.notes: The company is a debt purchaser/servicer; the clearest direct cost line is Cost of Revenues - Other, which is explicitly labeled as a cost of revenues. Other operating expenses and personnel expenses are better treated as SG&A/overhead.

candidates.why: This is revenue, not an expense; should not be classified as SG&A expense.
Also income rather than expense; not SG&A cost.
Operating overhead bucket with clear expense amounts; good SG&A component (excluding separately identified D&A/impairments).
Residual operating expenses category, likely SG&A-type overhead.
Personnel costs for collections staff; overhead/operating expense, fits SG&A.
Core personnel overhead; classic SG&A component.
Duplicate-style label for other operating expenses; also SG&A component.

variables.notes: No single consolidated SG&A line exists, so SG&A must be represented via multiple overhead components. Selected rows are operating expense buckets (personnel and other operating expenses) and explicitly exclude totals, balancing items, COGS, D&A and financial items.

candidates.why: Represents capitalized development assets, not a period R&D expense line; this company is a financial/collections business, so this is unlikely to be recurring R&D.

variables.notes: There is no explicit research & development expense line in the income statement. The available candidate (Mine & Other Development Assets) is a balance sheet asset and not an R&D expense, so XRD is treated as zero.

candidates.why: Interest income, not interest expense; should not be used for XINT which tracks expense.
Generic interest expense line; appears empty in preview but could be used in some historical periods.
Clearly labeled interest expense on borrowings with populated values; primary interest expense line.
Portion of profit attributable to non-controlling shareholders, not interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests, not interest expense.
Same concept; not interest expense.

Asset, not interest expense.

candidates.why: Debt principal, not interest expense.
Debt principal, not interest expense.
Equity, not interest expense.
Equity, not interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: XINT is represented as a priority list of expense lines. Interest expense on borrowings is the main recurring interest expense; lease-related interest is a supplemental component; a generic Interest Expense line is included as a fallback for periods where the more specific label might be missing.

candidates.why: Income statement share of profit from associates; not an equity stock measure.
Also income from equity-accounted investments, not total book equity.
Investment asset line, not total equity.
Component of equity, but not total shareholders’ equity.
Subcomponent of equity (reserves), not total equity.
Represents equity attributable to shareholders of the parent (since Non-controlling interests is presented separately and Equity + NCI ≈ Total equity); best BE choice without including NCI.
Includes non-controlling interests; would double-count MIB if used for BE.

variables.notes: The Balance Sheet presents Equity, Non-controlling interests, and Total equity. Numerical checks show Equity + Non-controlling interests ≈ Total equity, so Equity corresponds to equity attributable to owners/parent, which is the correct BE denominator component excluding MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to NCI; flows, not balance-sheet stock.
Same as above; profit allocation, not balance-sheet amount.
Duplicate naming; also a profit allocation line.
Unrelated asset; not minority interest equity.
Amortization; not minority interest.
Asset; not minority interest.
Balance sheet line for non-controlling interests in equity; primary MIB measure.
Alternative label for the same NCI balance; values mirror Non-controlling interests; good secondary choice.

variables.notes: MIB is taken from the balance sheet non-controlling equity lines. Both Non-controlling interests and Minority Interest are provided as a priority list, as they represent the same concept with identical values; later logic will pick the first available per year.
